seoTitle: How to Make a Wix Website Fit All Screens
metaDescription: Learn how to build a responsive Wix layout that scales cleanly across desktop, tablet, and mobile screens.
intro: A Wix site that looks great on desktop can easily break down on tablets or phones if you don't design with responsiveness in mind. This guide covers using layout gridlines, flexible strips, and the mobile editor to make sure your content scales properly across every screen size.

steps.heading: Check your horizontal gridlines
steps.body: Open your site editor and review the safety gridline indicators that mark your fixed layout boundaries.

steps.heading: Keep key elements within bounds
steps.body: Position headers, logos, and call-to-action buttons inside the safe width guidelines so they don't get clipped.

steps.heading: Use full-width strips and columns
steps.body: Deploy full-width layout strips and split content into flexible, proportional columns instead of fixed-size boxes.

steps.heading: Adjust image scaling
steps.body: Set your images to stretch or scale proportionally so graphics fit properly across different screen widths.

steps.heading: Switch to mobile view
steps.body: Toggle the mobile editor to recalibrate spacing, font sizes, and hide any redundant elements for smaller screens.

steps.heading: Preview across device sizes
steps.body: Use the live browser resizing preview to check your layout before publishing.

faq.question: Why does my content look clipped on smaller screens?
faq.answer: This usually happens when elements are positioned outside the safe width gridlines, so repositioning them inward often fixes it.

faq.question: Do I need to design mobile layouts separately?
faq.answer: Yes, Wix's mobile editor lets you adjust spacing and typography specifically for mobile, separate from your desktop design.

faq.question: Can I hide certain elements on mobile only?
faq.answer: Yes, the mobile editor includes hiding tools that let you remove cluttered elements from smaller screens without affecting desktop.

faq.question: How do I test responsiveness before publishing?
faq.answer: Use the live preview tool's resizing feature to check how your layout behaves across different screen widths before going live.
